description_long: This object is a tall potted palm plant with a slender, textured brown trunk and a crown of large, fan-shaped green leaves radiating outward. The trunk appears rough and fibrous, typical of palm species, and is relatively narrow compared to the wide spread of the foliage. The leaves are arranged in a dense, circular cluster at the top, with each leaf displaying multiple pointed segments. There is no visible pot or planter at the base, suggesting the trunk extends directly downward. The plant's overall appearance is lush and vibrant, making it suitable for decorative use in urban environments such as parks, plazas, or building entrances. No decorative elements or plant labels are visible on the trunk or foliage.
description: A tall, slender palm plant with a rough brown trunk and a dense crown of large, fan-shaped green leaves. The foliage is lush and radiates outward, with no visible pot or decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the palm, with the fan-shaped leaves symmetrically radiating from the top and the rough trunk clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the spread of the leaves and the narrowness of the trunk, with some leaves overlapping and extending outward.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the layered arrangement of the leaves and the fibrous texture of the trunk, with more leaf detail visible on the right.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the leaf cluster and the trunk, with the leaves appearing slightly denser and overlapping more at the top.
attribute_plant_species: fan palm (likely Washingtonia or Chamaerops species)
